  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",

    "installed-defective"
  ],
  "percent_complete": 50,

  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [
    "isolate-drain-sewer",
    "test-atmosphere-for-combustibles",
    "cover-seal-drain-opening",
    "issue-hot-work-permit",
    "establish-fire-watch",
    "commence-hot-work"
  ],
  "workable": [
    "isolate-drain-sewer",
    "test-atmosphere-for-combustibles",
    "cover-seal-drain-opening"
  ],
  "findings": [
    "Hot work is actively in progress directly over an unsealed/unprotected sewer or drain",
    "Failure to isolate and cover sewer/drain openings within 35 feet of hot work",
    "High risk of igniting accumulated flammable vapors or gases within the drainage system"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately and extinguish all ignition sources",
    "Evacuate the immediate area and monitor the atmosphere for combustible gases",
    "Seal and cover the drain/sewer opening with fire-resistant covers or wet blankets",
    "Re-evaluate and re-authorize the hot work permit before resuming operations"
  ],
  "rationale": "Performing hot work directly over an unprotected sewer or drain violates basic safety sequences. Under NFPA 51B and OSHA standards, all sewers, drains, and piping openings within a 35-foot radius must be covered, sealed, or isolated to prevent sparks from entering and igniting trapped flammable vapors before any hot work begins.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 51B Section 5.1.2.2",
    "OSHA 1926.352(c)",
    "OSHA 1926.352(i)"
  ]
}
